description_long: This is a large, double-headed sledgehammer with a robust, rectangular head made of gray stone or metal, featuring visible cracks and a weathered texture. The head is secured with metallic bolts and has a circular, dark brown cap on top, possibly for reinforcement. One side of the head displays a distinct emblem or logo, while the opposite side shows pronounced cracking, suggesting heavy use or age. The handle is cylindrical, dark brown to black, and appears to be made of wood or a composite material, with metallic rings reinforcing both ends. The overall construction is solid and utilitarian, designed for delivering powerful blows. The hammer's size and rugged build make it suitable for demanding urban tasks such as demolition or construction.
description: A heavy, double-headed sledgehammer with a cracked, gray stone or metal head and a reinforced dark handle, featuring a visible emblem on one side.
description_view_0: Front view showing the emblem on the hammer head, both striking faces, and the reinforced handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cracked surface of the head and the rectangular profile of the striking face.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, but with the emblem mirrored and bolts visible on the head.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing more pronounced cracks on the head and the rectangular striking face.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, cylindrical handle made of dark wood or composite, reinforced with metal rings, smooth with no visible grip enhancements.
attribute_head_type: Double-headed sledgehammer type with large, rectangular striking faces, made of gray stone or metal, featuring cracks and metallic bolts.
attribute_brand_or_logo: A circular emblem or logo is visible on one side of the hammer head.
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: Visible cracks and surface wear on the hammer head, especially on the sides.
